content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN.

Construct an array AAA of length 202020 with 1≤Ai≤1081 \le A_i \le 10^81≤Ai​≤108 such that the following condition holds:


For each i=1,2,…,Ni = 1, 2, \ldots, Ni=1,2,…,N, it is possible to find a subset of AAA of size **at most** 101010, such that the sum of elements is exactly iii.
There are only 222 tests, the sample with N=20N = 20N=20 and the hidden test with N=50000N = 50000N=50000.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
Output on a new line 202020 integers - A1,A2,…,A20A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_{20}A1​,A2​,…,A20​ (1≤Ai≤1081 \le A_i \le 10^81≤Ai​≤108), the required array.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111 : 1≤i≤101 \le i \le 101≤i≤10 can be constructed by just taking a singleton subset of that element.
11≤i≤2011 \le i \le 2011≤i≤20 can be constructed by taking a subset consisting of 101010 and i−10i - 10i−10.
